rct_screen_evidence: "Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel ... Patients were stratified according to age ..."
intervention: ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body‑surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles)

1.1.quote: "Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel..." (Treatment Plan, Stratification, and Randomization)
1.1.justification: The study states that patients were randomly assigned, and given the large, multicenter nature of the trial, a computer‑generated or similar random method is highly probable even though not explicitly described.

1.2.quote: "Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy..." (Treatment Plan, Stratification, and Randomization)
1.2.justification: While the exact concealment method is not detailed, the use of stratification and central randomization in a large cooperative group trial makes adequate allocation concealment probable.

1.3.quote: "Patient characteristics were well balanced between the two groups (Table 1)..." (Baseline Characteristics)
1.3.justification: The baseline table shows comparable distributions of age, race, performance status, disease volume, etc., indicating no evidence of a problem with the randomization.

2.1.quote: "We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel ... or ADT alone." (Methods)
2.1.justification: Allocation to distinct treatment regimens (chemotherapy plus ADT vs ADT alone) was explicit and no blinding procedures were described, making participants aware of their group.

2.2.quote: "We assigned men ... to receive either ADT plus docetaxel ... or ADT alone." (Methods)
2.2.justification: Healthcare providers administering docetaxel versus ADT alone would necessarily know the assigned intervention, and no blinding of personnel is reported.

3.1.quote: "An intention-to-treat analysis was conducted that included all randomly assigned patients regardless of eligibility and treatment status." (Statistical Analysis section)
3.1.justification: The authors explicitly state that all randomized participants were analysed, implying outcome data (including censoring) were available for virtually all.

4.1.justification: Progression-Free Survival is a standard, objective clinical endpoint typically measured using predefined radiographic and biochemical criteria.

4.2.quote: "Patients were assessed for progression according to protocol-defined criteria in both arms."
4.2.justification: Both intervention and control groups were monitored using identical follow‑up schedules and the same criteria for defining progression.

4.3.justification: Not applicable because measurement methods were appropriate and identical across groups.

4.4.justification: Per Sterne et al. 2019: knowledge is unlikely to influence vital-status or biomarker outcomes without judgment (e.g., all-cause mortality).

5.1.justification: Progression‑Free Survival was not listed in the registration, so the analysis was not pre‑specified.

5.2.quote: "The median time to biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic progression was 20.2 months in the combination group, as compared with 11.7 months in the ADT-alone group (hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.51 to 0.72; P<0.001)." (Results section)
5.2.justification: Only a single definition and time point for progression‑free survival is presented, indicating no selective measurement.

5.3.quote: "Cox proportional‑hazard models, stratified according to the factors described above, were used to estimate hazard ratios for time‑to‑event end points." (Statistical analysis plan)
5.3.justification: The analysis follows the pre‑specified Cox model approach; no alternative analyses are reported that would suggest selective reporting.

2.3.quote: "6 patients in the combination group did not start the assigned therapy" (Results) or "No relevant text found"
2.3.justification: Deviations were due to non‑initiation of treatment, not trial‑context driven crossovers or contamination.

2.6.quote: "An intention-to-treat analysis was conducted that included all randomly assigned patients regardless of eligibility and treatment status." (Statistical Analysis)
2.6.justification: The study explicitly states an ITT approach, which is appropriate for estimating the effect of assignment.

domain_rationales.D4: 4.1 and 4.2 are N/PN and 4.3 or 4.4 is N/PN -> Low
domain_rationales.D2: Part1=Low (Part1 Low condition met); Part2=Low (2.6=Y/PY (appropriate ITT analysis) -> Part2=Low)
